title: trifolium roussaeanum subsp. dichroanthum
intro: Trifolium roussaeanum subsp. dichroanthum, also known as dichroanthum clover, is a lesser-known subspecies of the Trifolium roussaeanum plant, which belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). The dichroanthum clover is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of dichroanthum clover include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Dichroanthum clover can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
